# Ordinals API Overview

The Magic Eden Ordinals API is designed to empower developers in creating innovative experiences to gather data for [https://magiceden.io/ordinals](https://magiceden.io/ordinals)
.

We offer these endpoints in the spirit of fostering composability and openness in the web3 space, extending the reach of this data to anyone desiring to build upon it. In return, if you utilize our data, we ask that you adhere to our Terms of Service.

Moreover, we make this API available free of charge and kindly request that you attribute Magic Eden on your website or within your app. When you leverage our API, please consider linking to the Magic Eden marketplace from the NFTs that you showcase, where it's appropriate.

Please be aware that our API is rate-limited and some endpoints are gated by an API key. For more info check out [Ordinals API Keys](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/ordinals-api-keys)

Clusters

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/ordinals-overview#clusters)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Mainnet: **api-mainnet.magiceden.dev/v2/ord** - this uses the real Ordinals Mainnet cluster, the tokens are real and this is consistent with the data seen on [https://magiceden.io/ordinals](https://magiceden.io/ordinals)

---

# Magic Eden Developer

Magic Eden Developer Home
=========================

 [![Solana Logo](https://solana.com/src/img/branding/solanaLogoMark.svg) Solana APIs](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview)
[![Bitcoin Logo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/4/46/Bitcoin.svg) Bitcoin APIs](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/ordinals-overview)
[![Ethereum Logo](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/7/70/Ethereum_logo.svg) EVM APIs](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/evm-api-overview)
 [🧪 Code Examples](https://docs.magiceden.io/recipes)
 [👨‍ Terms and Conditions](https://magiceden.io/legal-policies/api-terms)

Welcome to our Development Environment

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/#welcome-to-our-development-environment)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **API Capabilities**: Fetch data for NFTs, Runes, Collection, Wallets and more. Generate and execute trading endpoints to interact directly with the Magic Eden marketplace  
    
*   **Project Showcase**: Be inspired by our curated selection of example projects built using our API. Explore the innovative applications and success stories of developers like you. See the [top projects](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/overview#highlight-projects)
     from our NYC Hacker House Hackathon.
    
*   **Upcoming Works**: We are dedicated to improving your experience. Look forward to a smoother onboarding process and the release of our SDK for seamless API integration.
    

Happy building!

The Magic Eden Team

# Changelog

Changelog
=========

Added

[Solana - Introducing the AMM Pool API](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/mmm)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

over 2 years ago by Tallal Mirza

We are excited to announce the introduction of new endpoints that enable API users to interact with our Automated Market Maker (AMM), also known as MMM, pools. These endpoints provide a seamless way for users to engage with the liquidity pools and perform various actions.

Added

[Solana - Filtering and Sorting Enhancements](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

over 2 years ago by Tallal Mirza

We're excited to announce some major improvements to our Solana API endpoints with the introduction of sorting and filtering capabilities. These enhancements empower you to have greater control over the data you retrieve, making it easier to find the information you need efficiently. Read on to explore the details of these updates!

---

# Solana - Filtering and Sorting Enhancements

[Back to All](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog)

Added

We're excited to announce some major improvements to our Solana API endpoints with the introduction of sorting and filtering capabilities. These enhancements empower you to have greater control over the data you retrieve, making it easier to find the information you need efficiently. Read on to explore the details of these updates!

### 

Endpoint Updates

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#endpoint-updates)

#### 

1\. [`/v2/collections/{symbol}/listings`](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_collections-symbol-listings)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#1-v2collectionssymbollistings-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price, maximum price and NFT attributes.
*   Sort by the listing price and when the listing was last updated.

#### 

2\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/tokens`](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-tokens)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#2-v2walletswallet_addresstokens-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price, maximum price and NFT attributes.
*   Sort by the listing price and when the listing was last updated.

#### 

3\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/offers_received`](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-offers-received)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#3-v2walletswallet_addressoffers_received-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

#### 

4\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/offers_made`](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-offers-made)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#4-v2walletswallet_addressoffers_made-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

#### 

5\. [`/v2/tokens/{token_mint}/offers_received`](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_tokens-token-mint-offers-received)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#5-v2tokenstoken_mintoffers_received-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

Please refer to the [API documentation](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview)
 for each endpoint to learn more about the available sorting attributes and filter options.

### 

Conclusion

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#conclusion)

With the introduction of sorting and filtering enhancements, we've made it easier for you to retrieve and manipulate data from our API. These updates offer greater flexibility and control, enabling you to optimize your integration with our platform. We hope you find these improvements valuable and they enhance your overall experience. If you have any questions or feedback, please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team. Happy sorting and filtering!

---

# Solana - Introducing the AMM Pool API

[Back to All](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog)

Added

We are excited to announce the introduction of new endpoints that enable API users to interact with our Automated Market Maker (AMM), also known as MMM, pools. These endpoints provide a seamless way for users to engage with the liquidity pools and perform various actions.

Please refer to our [API documentation](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/mmm)
 to learn more! Also make sure to check out this explanation of [Pool Pricing](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/mmm-pool-pricing)
!

The endpoints will be accessible to anyone with an API key!

#### 

New Endpoints:

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/changelog/mmm#new-endpoints)

1.  [**`/v2/mmm/pools`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_mmm-pools)
     - GET: Get AMM pools corresponding to an owner or collection symbol.
    
2.  [**`/v2/mmm/token/{mint_address}/pools`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_mmm-token-mint-address-pools)
     - Get best offers for an NFT.
    
3.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/create-pool`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_instructions-mmm-create-pool)
     - GET: Get instruction to create a pool.
    
4.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-withdraw-buy`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-withdraw-buy)
     - GET: Get instruction to withdraw SOL payment from a pool.
    
5.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-fulfill-buy`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-fulfill-buy)
     \- GET: Get instruction to have a pool fulfill a buy.
    
6.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-fulfill-sell`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-fulfill-sell)
     - GET: Get instruction to have a pool fulfill a sell

---

# Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

Security Issues and Bug Bounties

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/frequently-asked-questions-faqs#security-issues-and-bug-bounties)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Magic Eden’s API and most other assets are covered under our bug bounty program with HackerOne. We invite you to explore our API and actively participate in identifying vulnerabilities. To submit an issue or learn more about our program, check out our [Bug Bounty Program](https://hackerone.com/magic-eden?type=teamour)
.

---

# Solana API Overview

The Magic Eden Solana API is designed to empower developers in creating innovative experiences using NFTs and our abundant marketplace data. We offer a robust suite of endpoints that allows you to access NFT metadata along with crucial components of our marketplace, including events, collections, listings, bids, and more. With our API, you can not only access the data but also perform various actions that you would on our user interface, such as listing NFTs, buying NFTs, placing bids, and more.

We offer these endpoints in the spirit of fostering composability and openness in the web3 space, extending the reach of this data to anyone desiring to build upon it. In return, if you utilize our data, we ask that you adhere to our Terms of Service.

Moreover, we make this API available free of charge and kindly request that you attribute Magic Eden on your website or within your app. When you leverage our API, please consider linking to the Magic Eden marketplace from the NFTs that you showcase, where it's appropriate.

Please be aware that our API is rate-limited and some endpoints are gated by an API key. For more info check out [Solana API Keys](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-api-keys)
.

Clusters

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview#clusters)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

More info about Solana clusters: [https://docs.solana.com/clusters](https://docs.solana.com/clusters)

*   Devnet: **api-devnet.magiceden.dev/v2** - this uses a testing Solana cluster, the tokens are not real
*   Mainnet: **api-mainnet.magiceden.dev/v2** - this uses the real Solana cluster, the tokens are real and this is consistent with the data seen on [https://magiceden.io/](https://magiceden.io/)
    

Highlight Projects

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview#highlight-projects)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can find a list of projects in analytics, trader tooling, and community management from our [NYC Hacker House Hackathon Gallery](https://magic-eden-api-hackathon.devpost.com/project-gallery)
.

##### 

The Winners in each respective category are:

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview#the-winners-in-each-respective-category-are)

*   Best Analytics Project: [Seller Classification](https://devpost.com/software/analytics-seller-classification)
    
*   Best Trader Tooling: [Solset.io](https://devpost.com/software/solset-io)
    
*   Best Social Media Integration: [Hawksight Bot Improvements via Magic Eden API](https://devpost.com/software/hawksight-x-magic-eden-api-hackathon)
    

API Metadata

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/reference/solana-overview#api-metadata)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Some endpoints in this API are able to respond with some useful metadata if requested. This is done by including the request header `ME-Pub-API-Metadata` and the value of this request header is a JSON object with the following TypeScript interface:

TypeScript

`interface RequestMetadata {   paging?: boolean }`

If an endpoint is requested with the header (and supports) `ME-Pub-API-Metadata`, the endpoint will respond with a header of the same name (`ME-Pub-API-Metadata`). The value of this response header is a JSON object with the following TypeScript interface:

typescript

`interface ResponseMetadata {   paging?: PagingMetadata }  interface PagingMetadata {   total: number   start: number   end: number }`

The below is an example

Request headers:  
`ME-Pub-API-Metadata: {"paging":true}`

Response headers:  
`ME-Pub-API-Metadata: {"paging":{"total":10, "start":0, "end":1}}`

---

# MMM Pool Pricing

_Tl;dr An automated market maker implies that instead of a human determining sell/buy prices, they are determined by various parameters we are going to walk you through below. 🙂_

_Now.. let’s talk about price calculation!_

Some definitions
================

*   > Liquidity Pool: simply put, a liquidity pool is a shared pot of digital assets (i.e. tokens)
    
*   > Spot price - _s_0: As defined in the pool
    
*   > Delta - _Δ_: As defined in the pool. Will be normalized to decimal value if the pool type is exponential
    
*   > All the below values should be normalized to decimal values for calculations (10% = 0.1, 100bps = 0.01, etc.)
    
    *   > Royalty percentage - _r_: As defined in the pool (for when the pool is buyer) or the input params (for when the pool is seller)
        
    *   > Seller fee basis points - _f_: As defined in metadata
        
    *   > Maker fee - _m_: Maker fee, as defined by our maker/taker fee structure
        
    *   > Taker fee - _t_: Taker fee, as defined by our maker/taker fee structure
        
    *   > Effective LP fee - _l_: conditional function where it is zero if the pool is one sided and taken from the pool data if the pool is two sided
        
*   > Effective price - _e_: What the person interacting with the pool expects to receive (if pool is buyer) or pay (if pool is seller)
    

Price updates
=============

With every trade that occurs, the spot price will update to better reflect the market conditions implied by a trade. After the pool **buys** a token, the price the pool is willing to pay for another token will be **lower**; similarly, after the pool **sells** a token, the price the pool is willing to sell another token will be **higher**.

In the below functions, only discuss how **spot price changes** with each trade, effective prices will be discussed later. We will define _s__n_ as the spot price after _n_ trades in a specific direction. If _n_ is positive, it will be after _n_ buy transactions, and if _n_ is negative, it will be after _n_ sell transactions. The graphs shown are also made such that once n becomes negative, it will be red and between parentheses.

Linear pools
------------

For linear pools, the delta is interpreted as lamports. For ease of representation, we show delta and spot price in SOL

_s__n_ = _s_0 − _n__Δ_

![Chart](data:image/png;base64,)

Exponential pools
-----------------

For exponential pools, the delta is interpreted as basis points. For ease of representation, we show delta in percentage.

s n   \=   s 0 ( 1 1   +   Δ ) n

![Chart](data:image/png;base64,)

Effective Price
===============

To enforce a spread between the buy and sell prices, we calculate effective price for selling with _s_−1instead of _s_0. However, the royalty, lp fee, and taker fee also contribute to the spread between the buy and sell prices.

Note: The pool will ignore user input _r_ values if the token is a pNFT or has OCP enforced royalties. For pNFT, we will always enforce full royalties, and for OCP tokens, we will either enforce full royalties, or use the dynamic royalty if available.

We need to determine if a pool is two-sided to decide whether to charge LP fees. We can determine if a pool is two-sided by looking at how much SOL is deposited in the pool and how many NFTs are deposited into the pool. If the SOL amount deposited is > spot price and the amount of NFTs deposited is > 1, then it is a two-sided pool.

Pool Buying (AKA Collection Offers)
-----------------------------------

_e_ = _s_0(1−_r__f_−_l_−_t_)

Pool Selling (AKA AMM Listings)
-------------------------------

_e_ = _s_−1(1+_r__f_+_l_+_t_)

Example graphs
--------------

The graphs below assume that the pool is two sided.

Given a pool where the curve type is exponential, _s_0 = 1.5, _Δ_ = 25%, _r_ = 50%, _f_ = 2%, ,_t_ = 1.5%, _l_ = 1%, the initial effective buy and sell prices are shown below. This shows that for example, after selling 2 tokens, the taker price to buy the 3rd taken will be around 3.03 SOL

![Chart](data:image/png;base64,)

For readability reasons, the red and blue lines do not extend past the 0, even though the pool can still buy NFTs after selling some. However, if the pool was to buy a token, we can simulate this by extending the lines to show what the effective buy/selling prices will be. After buying one token, the pool is willing to sell at approximately 1.5 SOL and buy at 1.15 SOL instead of the previous 1.94 SOL and 1.44 SOL.

![Chart](data:image/png;base64,)

Below is an example of a linear curve, with the same values as the exponential curve except for delta being 0.1 SOL

![Chart](data:image/png;base64,)

Buyable/Sellable Tokens
=======================

To find the number of NFTs to sell, it is just the number of NFTs that are deposited into the pool. The process to find the number of NFTs buyable is a little trickier, but the easiest way to do it is probably iteratively, as seen in the code snippet below. ![](data:image/png;base64,)

To do it in a non-iterative fashion, we can use the arithmetic and geometric sequence functions to find that if n is the number of NFTs buyable given a spot price and curve delta, and d is the amount deposited, then

*   > For linear pools: n \= ( 2 s + Δ ) \- ( 2 s + Δ ) 2 \- 8 Δ d 2 Δ
    
    *   > This is assuming that the deposited amount d is less than the amount needed to fulfill s Δ buys. Otherwise, n = d
        
*   > For exponential pools: we can define γ \= 1 1   +   Δ , then n \= l o g γ ( d ( γ \- 1 ) s + 1 )
    

Maker Fees
==========

Most of the above discussion does not involve maker fees. Adding maker fees to the current iteration of MMM will change the behavior in two ways

*   > Since the pool maker pays the maker fees from the amount deposited, the above calculations for NFTs buyable will change to replace s with s(1+m)
    
*   > The pool will become unsustainable. Since maker fees are not factored into the spot price, every time a trade occurs, the deposited payment will be lowered by the maker fee amount paid. This amount is not received upon the next trade on the opposite side, as what would usually happen with spot price.
    
    *   > We can change this with a new iteration of MMM where LP fees, which are always paid by the taker, can be used to fund maker fees.

---

# EVM API Overview

The Magic Eden EVM APIs are designed to empower developers in creating innovative experiences to gather data for for Ethereum and its L2 chains.

We offer these endpoints in the spirit of fostering composability and openness in the web3 space, extending the reach of this data to anyone desiring to build upon it. In return, if you utilize our data, we ask that you adhere to our Terms of Service.

Moreover, we make this API available free of charge and kindly request that you attribute Magic Eden on your website or within your app. When you leverage our API, please consider linking to the Magic Eden marketplace from the NFTs that you showcase, where it's appropriate.

Please be aware that our API is rate-limited and some endpoints are gated by an API key. For more info check out [EVM API Keys](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/evm-api-keys)

Clusters

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/evm-api-overview#clusters)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   Mainnet: **api-mainnet.magiceden.dev/v4/{API}** - this uses the Mainnet cluster, the tokens are real and this is consistent with the data seen on our ETH and its L2 marketplaces.

---

We are excited to announce the introduction of new endpoints that enable API users to interact with our Automated Market Maker (AMM), also known as MMM, pools. These endpoints provide a seamless way for users to engage with the liquidity pools and perform various actions.

over 2 years ago by Tallal Mirza

These enhancements empower you to have greater control over the data you retrieve, making it easier to find the information you need efficiently. Read on to explore the details of these updates!

# Solana - Introducing the AMM Pool API

[Back to All](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog)

Added

We are excited to announce the introduction of new endpoints that enable API users to interact with our Automated Market Maker (AMM), also known as MMM, pools. These endpoints provide a seamless way for users to engage with the liquidity pools and perform various actions.

Please refer to our [API documentation](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/mmm)
 to learn more! Also make sure to check out this explanation of [Pool Pricing](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/mmm-pool-pricing)
!

The endpoints will be accessible to anyone with an API key!

#### 

New Endpoints:

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/mmm#new-endpoints)

1.  [**`/v2/mmm/pools`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_mmm-pools)
     - GET: Get AMM pools corresponding to an owner or collection symbol.
    
2.  [**`/v2/mmm/token/{mint_address}/pools`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_mmm-token-mint-address-pools)
     - Get best offers for an NFT.
    
3.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/create-pool`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_instructions-mmm-create-pool)
     - GET: Get instruction to create a pool.
    
4.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-withdraw-buy`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-withdraw-buy)
     - GET: Get instruction to withdraw SOL payment from a pool.
    
5.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-fulfill-buy`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-fulfill-buy)
     \- GET: Get instruction to have a pool fulfill a buy.
    
6.  [**`/v2/instructions/mmm/sol-fulfill-sell`**](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_instructions-mmm-sol-fulfill-sell)
     - GET: Get instruction to have a pool fulfill a sell

---

# Solana - Filtering and Sorting Enhancements

[Back to All](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog)

Added

We're excited to announce some major improvements to our Solana API endpoints with the introduction of sorting and filtering capabilities. These enhancements empower you to have greater control over the data you retrieve, making it easier to find the information you need efficiently. Read on to explore the details of these updates!

### 

Endpoint Updates

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#endpoint-updates)

#### 

1\. [`/v2/collections/{symbol}/listings`](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_collections-symbol-listings)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#1-v2collectionssymbollistings-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price, maximum price and NFT attributes.
*   Sort by the listing price and when the listing was last updated.

#### 

2\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/tokens`](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-tokens)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#2-v2walletswallet_addresstokens-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price, maximum price and NFT attributes.
*   Sort by the listing price and when the listing was last updated.

#### 

3\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/offers_received`](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-offers-received)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#3-v2walletswallet_addressoffers_received-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

#### 

4\. [`/v2/wallets/{wallet_address}/offers_made`](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_wallets-wallet-address-offers-made)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#4-v2walletswallet_addressoffers_made-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

#### 

5\. [`/v2/tokens/{token_mint}/offers_received`](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/get_tokens-token-mint-offers-received)
 Endpoint

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#5-v2tokenstoken_mintoffers_received-endpoint)

*   Filter by minimum price and maximum price.
*   Sort by the bid amount and when the bid was last updated.

Please refer to the [API documentation](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/reference/solana-overview)
 for each endpoint to learn more about the available sorting attributes and filter options.

### 

Conclusion

[](https://docs.magiceden.io/v4.0/changelog/sorting-and-filtering-enhancements#conclusion)

With the introduction of sorting and filtering enhancements, we've made it easier for you to retrieve and manipulate data from our API. These updates offer greater flexibility and control, enabling you to optimize your integration with our platform. We hope you find these improvements valuable and they enhance your overall experience. If you have any questions or feedback, please don't hesitate to reach out to our support team. Happy sorting and filtering!

---
